description: Conversation for Action (CfA) state machine — a recursive 3-phase lifecycle for turning ideas into completed work.

intent.description: Intent Alignment: IDEA → INTENT. The intent-lead runs the intent-alignment skill to completion in a single invocation — DRAFT/ALIGN/ASK/REVISE/ASSERT are internal steps of the skill, not first-class states. The skill emits a terminal outcome (approve, withdraw) via .phase-outcome.json.

planning.description: Planning: INTENT → WORK_IN_PROGRESS. The project lead runs the planning skill to completion in a single invocation — DRAFT/ALIGN/ASK/REVISE/ASSERT are internal steps of the skill, not first-class states. The skill emits a terminal outcome (approve, backtrack, withdraw) via .phase-outcome.json.

execution.description: Execution: PLAN → COMPLETED_WORK. The project lead runs normal agent turns, dispatching workgroups via Send as needed, until WORK_SUMMARY.md is produced. The framework does not model task-level substates; subteam coordination happens over the message bus, not in the state machine.
